title: Intensely Charred Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A satvik-friendly Malaysian dish featuring charred and juicy chicken, boiled in a cataplana vessel with balsamic vinegar, this recipe is an energizing twist on traditional satay.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the cataplana vessel over medium heat.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the vessel and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the balsamic vinegar, coconut milk, lime juice, cumin, coriander, and salt to the vessel.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the vessel from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill over high heat until intensely charred, about 2 minutes per side.
Serve the satay hot with additional lime wedges on the side.
Garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions, if desired.
Let the satay rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
